methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $43M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Tevel Aerobotics is a Series B-stage company in Drones & Autonomous Systems that has raised $42.6M to date. The broader sector includes seven private peers—among them Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Manna Aero—but no direct public comparables are available for valuation anchoring.

A single methodology proved applicable to this analysis. The Stage-Progression method, using total capital raised ($43M) as a valuation proxy, applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-later-stage step-ups, yielding an implied valuation of $81M. The Peer-Median approach was not viable; while the Drones & Autonomous sector spans seven private firms, the narrow filtering required—same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier—returned fewer than three datapoints, making a reliable median calculation infeasible without introducing material selection bias.

The triangulated analysis, constrained to a single method, supports a valuation range of $69M (low) to $93M (high), with a median of $81M. This range reflects reasonable variance around the stage-progression output. Key uncertainties include the applicability of the 1.9x multiplier to Tevel's specific risk profile and market conditions, the absence of direct peer benchmarking for cross-validation, and inherent limitations of using total raised as a valuation proxy in an early-stage, capital-intensive sector. Additional data on comparable recent Series B or C financings in autonomous systems would materially strengthen confidence in this range.
